title: Verify ALIGNING steer sign fix on hardware
description: The Pi differential steering formula in aruco_navigator.py had inverted steer sign — l=fwd+steer,r=fwd-steer was producing right turns when bearing_err was negative. Fixed to l=fwd-steer,r=fwd+steer in commit 7e22693. Run robot at gate 0 and confirm it now turns LEFT toward the gate opening rather than into the inside post. Check no overshoot with steer_kp=0.3.
notes: Unable to test on track currently. test_bearing.py showing OK — steer logic looks correct in simulation.

title: Upload firmware v5 to Yukon via Thonny
description: FIRMWARE_VERSION=5 adds RESP_SPEED_L/R (IDs 25/26) so CMD_SENSOR reports actual post-correction motor speeds from Core 1. Committed in 35cf1b3 but NOT yet on the Yukon hardware. Must upload yukon_firmware_and_software/main.py using Thonny (tools/upload.py is broken).

title: Bearing test tool: web UI for IMU heading + bearing/power/time control
description: Need a standalone test tool (tools/test_bearing.py) to diagnose bearing-hold nav issues. Tool should: show live IMU heading, accept target bearing + power% + run-time inputs, run motors in AUTO+bearing-hold mode, stop on button or timeout. Uses same Robot+_YukonLink pattern as aruco_navigator.

title: Upload FIRMWARE_VERSION=4 main.py to Yukon via Thonny
description: The latest main.py (FIRMWARE_VERSION=4) with per-module motor telemetry (12 new RESP IDs, TYPE_MOD_TELEM=0x09) has NOT yet been uploaded to the physical Yukon RP2040. Must use Thonny — tools/upload.py is broken. Also upload bno085.py if needed.
close_reason: Confirmed via direct comparison: Yukon already running FIRMWARE_VERSION=4, matches Pi main.py exactly

title: Add motor direction inversion variables to Yukon firmware
description: Add MOTOR_INVERT_FWD_REV and MOTOR_INVERT_LR boolean constants to main.py so motor direction can be corrected at the firmware level without rewiring. These compensate for physical mounting orientation of motor modules.

title: Add INA237 readings to JSONL data log
description: INA237 power monitor readings (pi_input_voltage, pi_input_current, pi_input_power, pi_ina_ok) were stored in SystemState and exposed in the dashboards but not included in the JSONL data log snapshots. Added pi_ina_ok, pi_v, pi_i, pi_p to the system section of every data log record (null when INA237 is absent).
close_reason: Completed and pushed in session 2026-05-12 (commits d00f78b, 1d74b38, c84375b, 57d7c82)

title: Add applied motor speeds to JSONL data log
description: The _DataLogger._snapshot() telemetry section only logged commanded drive.left/right values. Firmware v5 reports actual post-bearing-correction speeds (applied_l/applied_r) via RESP_SPEED_L/R but these were not written to the JSONL data log. Added applied_l/applied_r to the telemetry section of every data log record (0.0 on older firmware, matching the GUI fallback behaviour).
close_reason: Completed and pushed in session 2026-05-12 (commits d00f78b, 1d74b38, c84375b, 57d7c82)

title: Fix hardcoded battery voltage thresholds in GUIs
description: Status bar voltage colour coding in robot_dashboard.py and ground_station.html used hardcoded 10.5/11.5 V instead of the [battery] chemistry×cells values already defined in robot.ini. Both GUIs now read batt_warn_v/batt_crit_v from the state dict (lipo 3S default: 10.5/9.9 V). ground_station_v2.py gains --batt-warn-v / --batt-crit-v CLI args and the fake-data low-voltage alarm also uses the configurable threshold.
close_reason: Completed and pushed in session 2026-05-12 (commits d00f78b, 1d74b38, c84375b, 57d7c82)

title: Add INA237 power monitor for Pi supply input
description: Adafruit INA237 (product 6340) on I2C1 (0x40) monitoring the 12V input to the DC-DC converter that powers the Pi. Uses adafruit-circuitpython-ina23x library. Needs: drivers/ina237.py wrapper, tools/test_ina237.py monitor tool, daemon SystemState fields (pi_input_voltage/current/power/ina_ok), dashboard system panel row, robot.ini [ina237] section.

title: Live test ground_station_v2.py on hardware
description: ground_station_v2.py needs to be tested on hardware to verify: (1) record idempotency — starting a recording when one is already running should be a no-op, (2) RC SG switch (3-position) correctly starts/stops recording. See project_todo_ground_station_test.md memory for context.

title: Calibrate cam_heading_offset for front cameras
description: cam_heading_offset is currently 0.0 (reverted from a previously measured 23.3°). To calibrate: set cam_heading_offset=0, aim robot dead-ahead at a tag, read bearing_err from the ALIGNING debug log, then set cam_heading_offset to MINUS that value. Do this for both front_left and front_right cameras. Update robot.ini with the correct value.
